prompt: Create a clean, high-end editorial product photograph for TaraHome, landscape 3:2. Use the attached official IKEA references as strict identity and geometry references for the current MYGGBETT door/window sensor. Show the real two-piece slim white rounded-end sensor correctly installed on the inside of a slightly open warm natural-oak interior door and matching wood frame, with the larger button-and-LED unit on the fixed frame and the narrow magnet aligned closely on the moving door. Preserve the exact recognizable proportions, seam, small round button, tiny dark LED, gently rounded top and bottom, and two-piece relationship; do not redesign or invent hardware. Warm late-morning residential light, pale cream wall, oak grain, subtle linen curtain edge, and a restrained dark-green houseplant softly out of focus in the background. Practical lived-in home, calm natural materials, realistic mounting scale, shallow depth of field, product crisp and prominent in the center-left safe area with enough breathing room for both full cover and 16:9 article-card crop. No people, no hands, no phone, no app screen. No words, letters, labels, logos, headline, title treatment, icons, arrows, network lines, routing paths, diagrams, charts, graphs, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, decorative overlays, fake controls, or extra sensors. Photorealistic editorial lighting, honest product scale, no generic sci-fi smart-home look.
